J.reasoning: Two agents announced 0, one will match my choice (catch J fish), and the remaining player can catch at most 5. If I take 5 then worst-case total = 5 (me) + 5 (mirror) + 5 (remaining) + 0 + 0 = 15, avoiding collapse while maximizing my catch.

One agent will match my catch, others announced 0 and 1, and one player’s choice is unknown. If I pick 4, worst-case total = 2*4+1+0+5 = 14 ≤ 15, so I safely get 4 fish. Picking 5 risks collapse if the unknown picks 5.

Given only two clear announcements (0 and 2) and one ambiguous pledge, picking 3 balances upside and collapse risk: it yields a decent payoff if others stick to modest numbers while keeping total less likely to exceed 15 under uncertainty.

One announcement said they'd copy J, plus commitments of 0 and 3 are public and two players are unknown. To avoid risking collapse if unknowns pick high, choosing 1 keeps total ≤15 even in worst-case (mirror copies me and two unknowns pick 5). This is a safe strategy to secure a positive payout.
One agent pledged to match my catch, another said 4, one said 0; one player unknown. Picking 3 keeps total likely ≤15 in realistic/robust scenarios (avoids collapse) while giving a solid payout.

One agent promised to match my catch, one said 0, one said 5, and one is unknown. In the worst case the unknown takes 5, so choosing 2 yields total 14 (<=15) and gives me 2 fish; choosing 3 risks collapse.

One announcement implies someone will match my choice; another claims 0; another claims an impossible 6 (likely 5). To maximize my payoff while avoiding collapse if others also play high, choose a moderate 3 fish—good yield if total stays ≤15 and less risky if someone mirrors me.

Announcements are inconsistent and some claims exceed the 0–5 limit, so others are unreliable. Picking 3 balances risk and reward: it yields a positive payoff if total stays ≤15 while reducing collapse risk compared with choosing 4–5.

Other announcements imply the other fishermen together will take 8 (one said 0, one matches me, and totals given sum to 8). With 5 of mine the grand total is 13 ≤ 15 so the lake won’t collapse and I maximize my take.

One agent promised 0, another indicated others total 9, and one said they would match J. Choosing 2 keeps likely total ≤15 even if matched (0+9+2=11 or + match 2 →13), avoiding collapse while giving a reasonable personal payoff.
Announcements indicate others will take 10 fish total (plus one taking 0), leaving room for me to take the remaining 5 without collapsing the lake, maximizing my payout.
Announcements are unreliable (one claims 11 which is impossible, one says 0, one references my choice). To avoid high collapse risk while keeping decent payoff, choose a moderate 3 fish.

Compromise between maximizing my catch and avoiding collapse. If others choose moderately (around 3 each), total stays ≤15; choosing 3 gives a solid individual payoff while limiting risk of pushing the lake over 15.
Announcements are inconsistent/unreliable (one claims 13 which exceeds per-player limit). To maximize my payout given uncertainty, I take the dominant short-term gain and catch the maximum (5).
One announcement implies others will take 14 fish total; to avoid collapse (total >15) I should take at most 1. Taking 1 maximizes my gain without risking collapse if others keep their announced 14.

At worst the announcements are unreliable (one claims 15, impossible) and one says 0; to maximize my payout I take the maximum (5). If others keep their announced low/ambiguous catches I gain most; if they push total over 15 everyone loses, which I can't control.
With five players the safe average to avoid collapse is 3 each (5×3=15). Announcements are unreliable, so choosing 3 maximizes my guaranteed payout without unnecessarily risking collapse.

Maximize my payoff: if total ≤15 I want the highest personal catch. The announcement of 17 is impossible, so others likely won't reliably force collapse; choosing 5 yields highest return unless the lake collapses regardless.
Dominant strategy: maximize my own catch since others may overfish and collapse yields 0 for everyone anyway. Taking 5 maximizes my payout if the lake does not collapse.

One announced 0 and one will mimic my choice; another overclaimed 19 (likely max 5). Assuming the remaining player takes 5, total ≈ 2+2+0+5+5=14 ≤15. Choosing 2 maximizes my catch without risking collapse.
